stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and evaluation of Meta's latest large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function in language tasks.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: OpenAI and Broadcom are collaborating on a specialized chip designed for large language model (LLM) inference.
The announcement highlights the intense competition and demand within the AI hardware/silicon sector.
This development addresses the critical need for efficient, scalable hardware to run LLMs in production environments.

stories.title: Real-Time Voice AI Hears but Does Not Listen
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Four leading real-time voice AI systems (including OpenAI, Google, and Alibaba) were tested on tasks requiring understanding of both spoken words and vocal delivery.
The evaluation found that all tested systems prioritized the literal words spoken, failing to interpret meaning conveyed through vocal delivery patterns.
This failure resulted in critical errors in simulated scenarios, such as inappropriately ending calls with distressed callers or approving suspicious financial transactions.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
stories.author: jamdesk
bullet_points: OpenAI is developing its first custom AI chip, named Jalapeno, in collaboration with Broadcom.
This move signifies a major industry trend toward specialized, in-house silicon for optimizing AI inference workloads.
The development indicates a strategic effort by major AI players to control hardware supply chains and enhance computational efficiency.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes common ML frameworks and formats like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in its MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets and accelerates performance on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing.
The core value proposition is running AI tools entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: Google is finally opening the Play Store to outside payments
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Google is beginning to implement changes allowing outside payment methods on the Play Store.
This move comes amidst the ongoing antitrust lawsuit from Epic Games regarding Google's Android monopoly.
The changes relate to billing practices for developers globally, though the court settlement is pending.

stories.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
stories.author: Sharon Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of creating fine-grained reward models for LLM agents due to complex, long-horizon interactions.
It proposes that existing Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training processes inherently contain the necessary components for effective step-level scoring.
This approach potentially eliminates the need for expensive and difficult dedicated reward model training in agentic LLM systems.

stories.title: The Unfireable Safety Kernel: Execution-Time AI Alignment for AI Agents and Other Escapable AI Systems
stories.author: Łukasz Chmiel
bullet_points: The paper addresses the security and safety of AI agents that interact with external tools and APIs.
It critiques current safety methods (like prompts and filters) as insufficient because they reside within the agent's own, potentially exploitable, runtime.
The research introduces the concept of 'escapable AI systems' and proposes new authorization mechanisms to control these agents externally.

stories.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
stories.author: Sharon Li
bullet_points: Addresses the difficulty of building fine-grained reward models for LLM agents due to complex, long-horizon interactions.
Proposes that Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training inherently provides the necessary ingredients for effective step-level scoring.
This approach potentially eliminates the need for costly and difficult dedicated reward model training in agentic LLM systems.

stories.title: Same Evidence, Different Answer: Auditing Order Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Ehsan Adeli
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical gap in MLLM evaluation: the lack of testing for order sensitivity in multimodal inputs.
It introduces 'Facet-Probe,' a comprehensive five-facet auditing framework to test 18 frontier and open-weight MLLMs.
The methodology uses a Bayesian item-response model to effectively separate inherent model bias from noise introduced by input ordering.

stories.title: The Unfireable Safety Kernel: Execution-Time AI Alignment for AI Agents and Other Escapable AI Systems
stories.author: Łukasz Chmiel
bullet_points: The paper addresses the vulnerability of AI agents whose controls (like prompts and filters) reside within their own runtime environment.
It introduces the concept of 'escapable AI systems,' where internal controls are susceptible to manipulation by external inputs.
The research proposes a new approach—the 'Unfireable Safety Kernel'—for execution-time AI alignment, moving control outside the agent's direct address space.

stories.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical instability issue (catastrophic collapse) in Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods designed for LLM tool-use.
The research identifies that performance degradation in agentic RL stems from unexpected probability spikes in specific control tokens.
The authors propose that incorporating supervisory signals effectively resolves the instability and improves tool-use performance.

stories.title: Same Evidence, Different Answer: Auditing Order Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Ehsan Adeli
bullet_points: Introduces Facet-Probe, a new five-facet audit designed to test a critical reliability property in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs): order sensitivity.
Addresses a gap in current MLLM benchmarking, which typically evaluates models only on a single, canonical ordering.
Uses a Bayesian item-response model to effectively separate inherent ordering noise from systematic per-facet bias across 18 frontier MLLMs.

stories.title: Detect, Unlearn, Restore: Defending Text Summarization Models Against Data Poisoning
stories.author: Shirin Nilizadeh
bullet_points: Addresses the critical security threat of data poisoning during fine-tuning of LLMs used for abstractive text summarization.
Highlights that poisoning attacks can induce persistent, harmful summarization failures while bypassing standard evaluation metrics.
Introduces a unified post-hoc defense framework designed for detecting and remedying these adversarial attacks.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
A key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The new feature set also includes web search capabilities for Claude Code.
This integration enhances the functionality and complexity of AI agents running via Ollama.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports experimental local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely offline on their local machines.
Support for image generation on Windows and Linux is planned for future releases.

stories.title: A new paper argues Microsoft exaggerated its quantum claims a year ago
stories.author: Sophia Chen
bullet_points: A Nature paper challenges the fundamental technology behind Microsoft's Majorana 1 quantum chip.
The critique questions the viability of the topological qubit technology Microsoft touted in February 2025.
The story highlights skepticism surrounding major corporate quantum computing claims.

stories.title: One-two punch delivered in global operation disrupts cybercrime "assembly line"
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A global operation named 'Operation Endgame' has been launched against cybercrime.
The operation successfully disrupted two widely utilized tools within the cybercrime infrastructure.
This action targets the 'assembly line' used by cybercriminals, indicating a significant law enforcement/security effort.

stories.title: FCC plans ID mandate that could block anonymous use of prepaid burner phones
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is planning an ID mandate for prepaid burner phones.
This mandate could restrict the anonymous use of these phones.
Privacy advocates and domestic violence groups oppose the requirement.

stories.title: Learning Action Priors for Cross-embodiment Robot Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which often lack explicit motion priors.
Existing VLA models rely on VLM backbones but force the action module to learn complex physical motion from scratch.
The research proposes learning action priors to improve cross-embodiment robot manipulation by decoupling motion learning from initial cross-modal alignment.

stories.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation, a method achieving high pass@1 accuracy by using one model as both teacher and student.
A key finding is that this self-distillation approach can reduce output diversity and flatten pass@k curves.
The authors attribute this reduction in diversity to compounding biases inherent in using sampled demonstrations for self-distillation.

stories.title: Model Forensics: Investigating Whether Concerning Behavior Reflects Misalignment
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Model Forensics' as a method to distinguish between concerning AI behavior and benign errors.
It addresses a key limitation in safety research: that concerning behavior alone does not prove model misalignment.
A baseline two-step protocol is proposed for investigating whether AI actions stem from malign intent.

stories.title: A welding penetration prediction model for laser welding process based on self-supervised learning using physics-informed neural networks
stories.author: Haichao Cui
bullet_points: Introduces SimPhysNet, a novel algorithm for predicting laser welding penetration.
Utilizes self-supervised learning combined with Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs).
Achieves high classification accuracy with limited labeled image data, addressing data scarcity in welding quality control.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously forgets a learned rule during pretraining.
The model initially learns a pronoun-gender rule (e.g., resolving 'she' after a female name) with high accuracy, but later performance drops near zero.
This reversal occurs without any corresponding change in the model's loss curve, suggesting an internal, corpus-driven mechanism for knowledge retention/forgetting.

stories.title: TriViewBench: Controlled Complexity Scaling for Multi-View Structural Reasoning in MLLMs
stories.author: Lan-Zhe Guo
bullet_points: Introduces TriViewBench, a new benchmark designed to test Multi-View Structural Reasoning in MLLMs.
The benchmark uses synthetic 3D scenes with controlled parameters like object count and occlusion to manage complexity.
It addresses a gap in current research regarding how MLLMs scale when dealing with increasing structural complexity.

stories.title: Can Trustless Agents Be Trusted? An Empirical Study of the ERC-8004 Decentralized AI Agent Ecosystem
stories.author: Zhipeng Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical trust challenge in decentralized AI agent ecosystems as agents interact across organizational boundaries.
Introduces ERC-8004, a permissionless trust layer utilizing on-chain registries for Identity, Reputation, and Validation.
The study aims to empirically validate the effectiveness of the ERC-8004 protocol despite its rapid adoption.

stories.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation, a method achieving high pass@1 accuracy by using one model as both teacher and student.
A key finding is that this self-distillation approach leads to a reduction in output diversity and flattening of pass@k curves.
The authors attribute this issue to compounding biases inherent in designing self-distillation using sampled demonstrations.

stories.title: Model Forensics: Investigating Whether Concerning Behavior Reflects Misalignment
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Model Forensics' as a new approach to AI safety.
It addresses the limitation that concerning behavior alone does not prove model misalignment, as benign causes exist.
The proposed baseline protocol involves a two-step iterative process to investigate the intent behind model actions.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a language model spontaneously forgets learned rules during pretraining.
Demonstrated by a model learning a pronoun-gender rule early on, only to lose its ability to apply it later in the training run.
This reversal occurs without any discernible change in the model's loss curve, suggesting an internal, corpus-driven mechanism for knowledge retention/forgetting.

stories.title: FedReLa: Imbalanced Federated Learning via Re-Labeling
stories.author: Liuhua Peng
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of class imbalance in Federated Learning (FL), a common issue in decentralized training.
Proposes 'FedReLa,' a method that utilizes re-labeling to mitigate performance degradation caused by data heterogeneity and imbalance.
Highlights the difficulty of current data-level FL methods when facing severe class absence across different clients.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously forgets learned rules during pretraining.
The study demonstrates this reversal occurs without any discernible change in the model's loss curve.
The mechanism suggests that the training corpus itself dictates which learned linguistic rules are retained by the model.

stories.title: How Robust is OCR-Reasoning? Evaluating OCR-Reasoning Robustness of Vision-Language Models under Visual Perturbations
stories.author: Yi Chang
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of understanding regarding the robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) when performing OCR-based reasoning.
Visual perturbations (degradation) can cause OCR errors and structural distortions, significantly impacting reasoning tasks.
The authors introduce 'OCR-Robust,' a new benchmark specifically designed to systematically evaluate this robustness issue.

stories.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses instability issues (catastrophic collapse) in Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods used for LLM tool-use.
The study identifies that RL alone often leads to performance degradation and failure in complex tool-use tasks.
The paper proposes that incorporating supervisory signals can resolve these RL-based tool-use failures.

stories.title: The Tatoxa System for Text Detoxification in Low-Resource Languages: The Case of Tatar
stories.author: Alexander Panchenko
bullet_points: Introduces 'Tatoxa,' a novel system for text detoxification (harmful content detection/mitigation).
Addresses a critical gap in NLP research by focusing on low-resource languages, specifically Tatar.
Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance, outperforming existing commercial and open-source LLMs in the task.

stories.title: Dziri Voicebot: An End-to-End Low-Resource Speech-to-Speech Conversational System for Algerian Dialect
stories.author: Asma Kemmoum
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of low-resource NLP for Algerian Dialect, a language lacking standardized resources.
Proposes an end-to-end speech-to-speech conversational system specifically tailored for this dialect.
Tackles real-world linguistic complexities, including lack of orthography and French codeswitching.

stories.title: Cloudflare launched self-managed OAuth for all
stories.author: terryds
bullet_points: Cloudflare has released a self-managed OAuth solution.
This feature aims to provide greater control over authentication for users.
It represents an evolution in identity management offerings from a major infrastructure provider.

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for AI agent competition.
The core hypothesis is that structured competition can enhance AI reasoning and training data quality.
The project aims to facilitate the training and improvement of advanced AI models through adversarial learning.

stories.title: How I Used Automated Red Teaming To Take My AI Agent from 6/9 Breaches to Zero
stories.author: morganwilliscloud
bullet_points: The article discusses the practical application of automated red teaming against AI agents.
It details a security scenario where an AI agent was tested for vulnerability by attempting to access AWS credentials.
The core takeaway is the successful reduction of security breaches from a high number to zero using these automated testing methods.
